subUnits.title: 2.1 Periodic Law and Blocks of Elements
notes: The properties of elements are periodic functions of their atomic numbers.
When elements are arranged in order of increasing atomic number, elements with similar properties recur at regular intervals.
• Elements arranged in order of increasing atomic number
• Horizontal rows are called PERIODS (7 periods)
• Vertical columns are called GROUPS (18 groups)
• Elements in the same group have similar chemical properties
Elements are classified into four blocks based on the subshell in which the last electron enters:
• Group 1: Alkali metals (Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr)
• Group 2: Alkaline earth metals (Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra)
• Group 17: Halogens (F, Cl, Br, I, At)
• Group 18: Noble gases (He, Ne, Ar, Kr, Xe, Rn)
• Examples: Fe, Cu, Zn, Cr, Mn, Ni
• Placed separately at the bottom of periodic table
• Elements with properties intermediate between metals and non-metals
• Located along the zig-zag line in the periodic table
• Examples: Boron (B), Silicon (Si), Germanium (Ge), Arsenic (As), Antimony (Sb), Tellurium (Te)

quiz.q: The modern periodic law states that properties of elements are periodic functions of their:
quiz.explanation: The modern periodic law states that properties of elements are periodic functions of their atomic numbers, not atomic masses.

quiz.q: Elements in the same group of the periodic table have:
quiz.explanation: Elements in the same group have similar chemical properties because they have the same number of valence electrons.

quiz.q: The s-block elements include groups:
quiz.explanation: s-block elements are in Groups 1 (alkali metals) and 2 (alkaline earth metals), plus Helium.

quiz.q: Which block contains the transition metals?
quiz.explanation: d-block elements (Groups 3-12) are called transition metals.

quiz.q: The general electronic configuration of p-block elements is:
quiz.explanation: p-block elements have the general configuration ns² np¹⁻⁶ where the last electron enters the p-orbital.

quiz.q: Which of the following is a metalloid?
quiz.explanation: Silicon (Si) is a metalloid with properties intermediate between metals and non-metals.

quiz.q: The f-block elements are also called:
quiz.explanation: f-block elements (Lanthanides and Actinides) are called inner transition metals.

quiz.q: Which group contains the halogens?
quiz.explanation: Group 17 contains the halogens: Fluorine (F), Chlorine (Cl), Bromine (Br), Iodine (I), and Astatine (At).

quiz.q: Transition metals are characterized by:
opts: Forming colored compounds and complex ions
quiz.explanation: Transition metals form colored compounds, exhibit variable oxidation states, and form complex ions.

quiz.q: The noble gases are found in:
quiz.explanation: Group 18 contains the noble gases: Helium (He), Neon (Ne), Argon (Ar), Krypton (Kr), Xenon (Xe), and Radon (Rn).

subUnits.title: 2.2 Families of Elements (Groups IA-VIIIA)
notes: Elements: Lithium (Li), Sodium (Na), Potassium (K), Rubidium (Rb), Cesium (Cs), Francium (Fr)
• Soft metals that can be cut with a knife
• Low density (Li, Na, K float on water)
• Highly reactive - reactivity increases down the group
• React vigorously with water to form alkaline solutions
• Stored under oil to prevent reaction with air and moisture
Elements: Beryllium (Be), Magnesium (Mg), Calcium (Ca), Strontium (Sr), Barium (Ba), Radium (Ra)
• Harder and denser than alkali metals
• Higher melting points than alkali metals
• Reactive but less reactive than alkali metals
• React with water (except Be and Mg with cold water)
• Burn in air to form oxides
Elements: Fluorine (F), Chlorine (Cl), Bromine (Br), Iodine (I), Astatine (At)
• Exist as diatomic molecules (F₂, Cl₂, Br₂, I₂)
• Fluorine is the most reactive element
• Physical state changes down the group:
• React with metals to form salts (halides)
Elements: Helium (He), Neon (Ne), Argon (Ar), Krypton (Kr), Xenon (Xe), Radon (Rn)
• Boiling point increases down the group
• Helium used in balloons and as coolant
• Argon used in welding as inert atmosphere
• Reactivity: increases for metals, decreases for non-metals

quiz.q: Alkali metals are found in Group:
quiz.explanation: Alkali metals (Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr) are in Group IA of the periodic table.

quiz.q: The electronic configuration of alkali metals is:
quiz.explanation: Alkali metals have one electron in their outermost s-orbital: ns¹

quiz.q: Which alkali metal is the most reactive?
quiz.explanation: Reactivity of alkali metals increases down the group. Cesium (Cs) is more reactive than Li, Na, and K.

quiz.q: Alkaline earth metals form ions with charge:
quiz.explanation: Alkaline earth metals (Group IIA) lose 2 electrons to form M²⁺ ions.

quiz.q: Halogens exist as:
quiz.explanation: Halogens exist as diatomic molecules: F₂, Cl₂, Br₂, I₂

quiz.q: The most reactive halogen is:
quiz.explanation: Fluorine is the most reactive halogen and the most reactive non-metal element.

quiz.q: Reactivity of halogens _______ down the group.
quiz.explanation: Reactivity of halogens decreases down the group from F to I due to increasing atomic size.

quiz.q: Noble gases are unreactive because they have:
quiz.explanation: Noble gases have complete valence shells (stable octet), making them chemically inert.

quiz.q: Which noble gas is used in balloons?
quiz.explanation: Helium is used in balloons because it is lighter than air and non-flammable.

quiz.q: When sodium reacts with water, the products are:
quiz.explanation: 2Na + 2H₂O → 2NaOH + H₂. Sodium hydroxide and hydrogen gas are produced.
